title: dentaria douglassii
intro: Dentaria douglassii, also known as Douglass's toothwort or limestone penny cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to North America and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbalism. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Dentaria douglassii can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and its roots and leaves can also be dried and used in tinctures or capsules.
